body:
The S1API uses the old (non-SDK) project format with ToolsVersion="15.0" and Microsoft.CSharp.targets. This format does not support .NET 6 it is intended only for .NET Framework. Manually setting <TargetFrameworkVersion>v6.0</TargetFrameworkVersion> with <TargetFrameworkIdentifier>.NETCoreApp</TargetFrameworkIdentifier> does not work.

Maybe for the next update with net 6 compability?!

edit: netcore6 works
title: net 6.

body: Right now, setting up the unlock flow for dealers (recommendation) isn’t very straightforward. You have to set the unlock type and also need to manually call Customer.RecommendDealer. Also, this only works with modded NPCs the API doesn’t allow editing in-game NPCs directly.

labels.name: Low
labels.description: Very minor issue, non gameplay hindering, fix all high prio first

body: Hi, I was looking at the current Server Browser UI and while it is quite powerful, it feels a bit heavy as an initial starting point, especially when you just want to get connected quickly. I tried setting up the buttons and loading a list of recently joined servers, which I am already storing locally: ### Thought / idea Why start with a full server browser right away? It might be nice to start smaller, for example: * Direct Connect (IP or address input) * A simple list of recently joined servers This could help lower the initial complexity, make the first experience smoother, and still allow the UI to grow into a more advanced browser later on. Just thinking out loud here, not saying the current approach is wrong. Curious how others feel about this direction.
title: Start with small features on Server Browser
